medications.drug_name: paracetamol for fever only, as a bridging measure
medications.indication_rationale: May be used for fever/discomfort while awaiting emergency transport, but does not replace the need for emergency assessment and treatment.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Sensitively assist with prompt removal of the tampon, position the patient supine with legs elevated, and continuously monitor her consciousness while awaiting the ambulance.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Removes the likely toxin source and supports blood pressure while awaiting emergency treatment.

patient_education_points.point: This looks like toxic shock syndrome — a bacterial toxin has gotten into your bloodstream, likely related to tampon use, causing your blood pressure to drop dangerously low.
Call 000 immediately for emergency assessment and treatment.
Remove the tampon now if you're able to safely do so.
Lie flat with your legs raised on some pillows while waiting for the ambulance.
In future, consider using lower-absorbency products and changing them within the recommended time frame to reduce this risk.

follow_up_plan.interval: Immediate emergency transport and hospital/ICU admission for IV fluid resuscitation and IV antibiotics
follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Blood pressure trend, organ function, and response to fluids and antibiotics
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Not applicable — already an emergency requiring immediate transport

must_include_items: Advice to remove the tampon promptly and sensitively
Recognition that this is toxic shock syndrome, not a simple viral illness
acceptable_alternatives: Paracetamol for fever as a bridging comfort measure only
errors_of_omission: No recognition of toxic shock syndrome
No advice to remove the tampon
errors_of_commission: Recommending only symptomatic treatment and review the next day
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Failing to recognise toxic shock syndrome and escalate immediately is a critical, potentially fatal safety failure.
